text.title: Edge-Case Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Edge-Case Checklist. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Checklist is exactly 10 cases: long-text, RTL, slow-network, no-data, error-state, offline, dark-mode, small-screen, a11y-screen-reader, time-zone. Custom additions go in a separate checklist.
rule.rationale: Open lists become aspirational and get skipped; 10 closed cases stay runnable in <10 minutes.

rule.statement: Each case is answered YES / NO / N/A with a one-line visual treatment note; vague 'maybe' or 'partial' answers are rejected.
rule.rationale: Binary answers force a clear treatment; 'partial' hides regret.

rule.statement: N/A answers carry a one-line reason ('component is read-only — no RTL input concern'); silent N/A is rejected.
rule.rationale: Silent N/A erodes the checklist; reasons make N/As auditable.

rule.statement: Any case with NO answer blocks handoff until either the design is updated to YES or the team explicitly accepts the debt (recorded in design-decision-log).
rule.rationale: Silent NO ships bugs to production; the block + decision-log path makes debt deliberate.

rule.statement: Checklist runs BEFORE design-to-dev handoff begins; mid-handoff checklist runs are rejected.
rule.rationale: Mid-handoff edge cases force rework; pre-handoff catch shifts the cost left.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do NOT run this methodology — emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition and route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above for flat methodologies), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-edge-case-checklist.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
